entries.title: The Mummy
entries.why: Lee Cronin’s reimagining should hit your smart, high-concept horror sweet spot.

entries.title: Backrooms
entries.why: Internet-myth horror with a strong concept hook and nasty atmosphere.

entries.title: Evil Dead Burn
entries.why: Reliable franchise horror with enough pedigree to be worth tracking.

entries.title: The Demon Road Trip
entries.why: André Øvredal’s new horror (Scary Stories to Tell in the Dark director) about a couple stalked by a demon after a road accident—smart, slow-burn dread with strong pedigree.

entries.title: Hokum
entries.why: Damian McCarthy’s Irish folk horror chiller (Oddity director) with Severance’s Adam Scott—creepy, atmospheric, and deeply unsettling.
